_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 9-21R Diltiazem (Revised 8/11/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template.
protocol_reference: Michigan Medication Protocol Section 9-21R: Diltiazem — MDHHS Approved 8/11/2023, Reviewed 2023
indications: Narrow complex tachycardia — irregular (atrial fibrillation or atrial flutter with rapid ventricular response)
contraindications: Sinus node dysfunction (sick sinus syndrome) without pacemaker
High-degree AV block (2nd degree Mobitz II or 3rd degree) without pacemaker
Wide complex tachycardia (do not use — may precipitate decompensation in V-Tach)
WPW (Wolff-Parkinson-White) with pre-excitation — may accelerate conduction through accessory pathway
precautions: Monitor BP and ECG continuously during and after administration
Can cause significant hypotension and bradycardia
Concurrent beta-blockers significantly increase risk of AV block and hypotension
Administer slowly to reduce hypotension risk
expected_effects: Reduction in ventricular rate in atrial fibrillation / flutter
Conversion of SVT to sinus rhythm (less reliable than adenosine)
notes: Administer slowly IV over 2-3 minutes to reduce hypotension
Standard adult dose: 15-20 mg (0.25 mg/kg) IV
No standard pediatric EMS dosing in Michigan protocol — consult online MC for pediatric use
Preferred over adenosine for rate control in atrial fibrillation/flutter

dosing.population: Adult (>14 yr) — narrow complex tachycardia (regular or irregular)
dosing.dose: 15-20 mg (0.25 mg/kg) IV slowly over 2-3 minutes
dosing.notes: Monitor BP and ECG during administration; stop if hypotension develops
